title: redb and AgentDB runtime boundaries defined
objective: Define the runtime mechanics of the two non-canonical tiers STORE-001 already assigned — redb as the transient high-frequency tier (buffering, deduplication, application-level WAL, ordered/idempotent/restartable/observable flush, per-role crash recovery) and AgentDB (.rvf) as portable, witness-bound per-agent cognition (MicroLoRA, SONA, FastGRNN, learning traces, witness facts, agent lifecycle) — so that neither store can silently become competing PostgreSQL macro authority.

work_items.owner: envctl bridge (single forward writer) over redb buffer
work_items.decision: transient durable-until-acked buffer; SHA-256 content identity; forward-only nu_plugin -> redb -> envctl -> PostgreSQL; drained in monotonic sequence; commit-once dedup at PostgreSQL; re-parsable source on loss
disposable cache keyed by BLAKE3(content+model+version+config); miss recomputes deterministically; never authority; derived from canonical PostgreSQL semantic-vector rows

durable-until-acked buffer, explicitly NOT PostgreSQL's WAL; monotonic sequence + per-operation idempotency key; FIFO replay with at-most-once apply on reconnect; subordinate to PostgreSQL; protocol defined, not yet built

work_items.owner: owning agent runtime (single writer per scratchpad)
work_items.decision: transient/session-scoped; agent-local key; discardable on crash; only envctl-curated significant states flush forward one-way; raw traces never replayed as authority
stateless zero-copy f32 cosine-distance accelerator; NO SQL / secondary index / native vector-search logic; authoritative semantic search stays in PostgreSQL + RuVector (No-Sidecar)

work_items.surface: redb shared UI state view over UDS (REDB-RT-06)
work_items.decision: read-only projection; UDS control path; must not become a dual-writer over agent state; view is disposable and re-read on restart

work_items.surface: AgentDB (.rvf) cognition — MicroLoRA, SONA, FastGRNN, learning traces, witness facts, agent lifecycle (AGENTDB-01..06)
work_items.owner: AgentDB per agent (non-authoritative for shared macro-state)
work_items.decision: portable single-file cognitive container hot-swappable across ruvllm nodes and regenerable from base model + history; every promoted fact SHAKE256 witness-bound + causal-edge-rooted in PostgreSQL source vectors; macro_authority 'none' for every entity

work_items.surface: durable-today redb uses PGREDB-001 (CodeDB source_blobs) and PGREDB-002 (envctl migration_db)
work_items.owner: governed by STORE-001-C, not by this family
work_items.decision: referenced, NOT reclassified — migrate to PostgreSQL only via the BlobStore SHA-256 parity cutover after a green live parity run; not silently promoted to transient nor frozen as durable

completion_gate: Buffer flush is ordered, idempotent, restartable, and observable; AgentDB identities and learning state are portable and witness-bound; neither store can silently become competing macro state — with every redb runtime role and every .rvf cognition entity assigned exactly once in generated/runtime_store_boundary_plan.json and POSTGRES-006.proof.json recording zero live redb/.rvf/PostgreSQL mutation.
next_action: Break buffering, cognition, routing, learning, and recovery into separate small implementation tasks after graph closure (per task_graph next_action).
